summary:
There is no documented evidence linking high-risk HPVs (such as types 16 or 18) to breast milk or breast pathology, despite general reports of HPV DNA being found in breast milk without subtype specificity. The absence of data supporting high-risk HPV detection—combined with known tissue tropism and pathogenesis patterns—maintains the original conclusion’s validity.  

\boxed{B}

\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
